outcome: You know the active constraints, portal status, and design-freeze target.

tasks.desc: Set up project folder structure: /01_rules, /02_CAD, /03_sim, /04_BOM, /05_media, /06_buildlog.
Verify Red Bull application page date/deadline and capture screenshots of live portal/rules.
tasks.question: What is the binding application deadline and event date?

tasks.desc: Write the project success target: validated Design Freeze v1 by 10 July, then build execution.

outcome: A one-page brief defines what must be true before cutting material.

tasks.desc: Write a one-page design brief: theme, driver story, engineering goals, crowd hook, and first-time-builder limits.
Translate rules into a design envelope: max size, max weight, gravity-only, steering, brakes, no batteries/effects.
Create validation criteria matrix: safety, steering, braking, mass, CoG, aero, buildability, spectacle.
tasks.question: Which criteria are go/no-go vs nice-to-have?

outcome: The driver, body, wheels, and ground clearance fit inside a realistic package.

tasks.desc: Collect reference images/dimensions for a domestic gas heater and LP gas cylinder; use real product proportions where possible.
Create driver/cockpit envelope: seated posture, helmet clearance, knees, steering reach, emergency exit space.
Sketch side and top layout: wheelbase, track width, seat position, body rake, ground clearance.
tasks.question: What are the initial wheelbase/track/clearance targets?

outcome: Baseline and fallback architectures are chosen before CAD detail.

tasks.desc: Compare chassis routes: shutterply platform, bolt-together angle frame, limited outsourced welding.
tasks.question: Which chassis route is safest and buildable with available tools?

tasks.desc: Compare steering/front-end routes: donor pedal kart, custom pitman steering, simple pivot axle fallback.
tasks.question: Can a donor go-kart front end be sourced quickly?

tasks.desc: Record architecture decision with fallback and risk controls.
tasks.question: Baseline architecture frozen for CAD v0?

outcome: CAD/CFD tools are ready and the workflow is clear.

tasks.desc: Set up Autodesk Fusion personal/education account and create the project workspace.
Create SimScale account and bookmark tutorials, public projects, and validation examples for external aerodynamics.
Update tracker habit: every work session ends by updating status, notes, blockers, and next action.

outcome: A parametric skeleton exists for fast iteration.

tasks.desc: Create Fusion parameters: wheelbase, track width, ground clearance, body rake, body height, body width.
Model simple proxy geometry: wheels, axles, driver cylinder, heater body box, seat, roll/side protection zones.
Export 3 screenshots: side, top, isometric for feedback/recruitment.

outcome: The first chassis model has mounting points and inspection-critical geometry.

tasks.desc: Model ladder/floor chassis with cross members, triangulation placeholders, axle mount points, and seat mounts.
Add steering column support, brake lever/cable zones, and wheel clearance keep-out regions.
Check dimensions against rule envelope and add a visible ground-clearance reference plane.

outcome: Complex shapes are simplified before simulations start.

tasks.desc: Convert chassis v0 into standard material assumptions: ply sheets, angle iron, bolts, gussets.
Mark every part as DIY, buy, salvage, or outsource; flag risky fabrication steps.
Simplify parts that would require precision welding or awkward compound cuts.
tasks.question: Which features should be simplified before FEA?

outcome: Steering and braking are designed early, not fitted as an afterthought.

tasks.desc: Model donor-style front axle, kingpin/pitman proxy, steering rod, steering column bracket, and foot/hand clearance.
Model brake lever, cable path, rear brake mount, and optional drag-brake zone.
Choose braking baseline: one rear wheel + drag brake, or two-wheel braking if parts allow.
tasks.question: What braking setup gives inspection confidence without overcomplexity?

outcome: The tall visual concept is checked against mass and stability risks.

tasks.desc: Create mass budget for chassis, body, wheels, axles, brakes, seat, steering, driver props, and ballast.
Set target cart mass: structure comfortably below 80 kg, with any ballast removable and low-mounted.
tasks.question: What is the pre-ballast mass target?

tasks.desc: Estimate CoG height/location using rough component masses; flag high/tall components for light materials.

outcome: The concept is application-ready and understandable in seconds.

tasks.desc: Create a clean concept render/sketch from CAD and the side-cutaway concept.
Draft 150–250 word concept pitch and 30-second skit outline.
Run the one-second test: show sketch to 2 people and ask what the vehicle is.
tasks.question: Does the concept read instantly as gas heater + cylinder?

outcome: Application and teammate recruitment move in parallel.

tasks.desc: Submit application if portal/deadline is available; otherwise complete all fields and log portal blocker.
tasks.question: Has the application been submitted before the live deadline?

tasks.desc: Post/send teammate call: driver/fabrication/CAD/performance roles, expected time commitment, concept image.
Create sponsor lead list: LPG, heaters, hardware/materials, with contact, ask, and status.

outcome: FEA and CFD variants are clean enough to run quickly.

tasks.desc: Create FEA-only CAD: chassis, mounts, seat loads, axle loads; remove cosmetic bodywork.
Define FEA load cases: static, braking, cornering, jump landing at 4–5× loaded weight.
tasks.question: What load cases are mandatory before build?

tasks.desc: Create CFD-only CAD: simple body/wheels/driver envelope; define baseline upright, raked, boat-tail variants.

outcome: You have the first structural evidence, even if it fails.

tasks.desc: Run a Fusion static stress tutorial or simple beam test to avoid setup mistakes.
Apply materials, constraints, and load cases to chassis v0; run FEA-01.
Capture max stress, max displacement, safety factor, and failed joints/screenshots.

outcome: Structural weaknesses become bracing changes, not scary surprises.

tasks.desc: Add bracing/gussets/reinforcement where FEA-01 shows stress concentration or excess deflection.
Rerun FEA-02 using the same load cases and compare against FEA-01.
Decision: approve bracing pattern, simplify further, or shift material strategy.
tasks.question: Does the chassis pass the safety-factor target, or does it need redesign?

outcome: You have a baseline drag/wake reference for the ugly upright-box case.

tasks.desc: Upload/import simplified upright model to SimScale or equivalent external aerodynamics setup.
Run CFD-01 upright heater baseline at consistent speed settings, e.g. 30, 45, 60 km/h if feasible.
Capture drag metric, pressure plot, wake plot, and mesh/setup notes.

outcome: Aero choices are selected by comparison, not vibes with wheels.

tasks.desc: Run CFD-02 raked body variant with the same settings as baseline.
Run CFD-03 raked + boat-tail/gas-cylinder pod variant with identical settings.
Compare drag/wake screenshots and choose next geometry tweak.
tasks.question: Which body shape gives the best buildable aero improvement?

outcome: The glow effect has a reliable legal primary route and a fallback.

tasks.desc: Sketch airflow-revealed panel, fabric flame, and manual reveal mechanisms.
Score each mechanism for legality, reliability, jam risk, build time, reset time, and crowd readability.
Decision: choose primary effect and backup; define how it cannot jam wheels/steering.
tasks.question: Airflow panels, fabric flames, or manual reveal?

outcome: The model, simulations, and decisions converge into one buildable version.

tasks.desc: Update master CAD with winning chassis, steering, brake, body, and effect decisions.
Review all open P0 decisions and blockers; close or assign next action.
Run Design Gate 1 review: rules, FEA, CFD, mass, steering/brakes, buildability, spectacle.
tasks.question: Is the design ready to turn into build drawings?

outcome: The design becomes a shopping/cutting/assembly package.

tasks.desc: Generate dimensioned drawings/cut list for chassis/floor/body panels/brackets.
Create assembly sequence: chassis -> axles -> steering -> brakes -> seat -> body -> effects.
Mark inspection-critical parts: steering column support, brake mount, axle mounts, ground clearance, battery-free effects.

outcome: You know what to buy, salvage, borrow, or fabricate before build starts.

tasks.desc: Find 3 donor go-kart/front-end candidates and 3 wheel/brake options; log prices and availability.
Update BOM with material quantities, cost estimate, supplier, and risk/availability notes.
Decision: first build purchase list and maximum spend before teammate/sponsor confirmation.
tasks.question: What can be bought now without risking wasted money?

outcome: A reviewer can understand the design without you narrating it.

tasks.desc: Assemble design pack: CAD screenshots, FEA report, CFD comparison, BOM, risk register, build sequence.
Ask 1–2 external reviewers to check for obvious safety/buildability issues.
Revise the design pack based on review comments; flag unresolved issues.

outcome: You have a validated, traceable, buildable design package.

tasks.desc: Complete the go/no-go checklist and record evidence for every P0 criterion.
tasks.question: Build now, revise, or pause?

tasks.desc: Back up files and tag Design Freeze v1: CAD, drawings, simulation logs, BOM, risk register.
Prepare build board and private build-log backlog; do not launch public updates until build starts and risk is controlled.

outcome: Workshop is safe, organized, and ready for cutting.

tasks.desc: Clear workspace, set up PPE, extension leads, storage bins, and safe cutting/assembly zones.
Buy or reserve first-round materials: ply/steel/fasteners/adhesive/correx/foam.
Start private photo log: before-build baseline, materials, workspace setup.

outcome: Donor steering/brake parts are usable or replacements are identified.

tasks.desc: Inspect donor kart/front-end candidates; check play, cracks, bent axles, bearing drag, and steering smoothness.
tasks.question: Buy donor now or keep searching?

tasks.desc: Create parts-cleaning and bearing-service list.

outcome: You can cut without guessing.

tasks.desc: Print or draw full-scale chassis/floor template on cardboard/ply; mark centreline and axle lines.
Review fastener sizes, washers, locknuts, bracket/gusset quantities.

outcome: Build support moves without blocking engineering.

tasks.desc: Send sponsor outreach batch 1 with concept image, ask, and non-guaranteed approval caveat.
Follow up with potential teammates; assign provisional roles even if people are part-time.
tasks.question: Who owns build, CAD updates, procurement, and performance?

outcome: Frame/floor cuts are queued in the right order.

tasks.desc: Mark and cut non-critical floor/chassis pieces first; leave safety-critical brackets until parts are confirmed.
Dry-fit chassis pieces on flat floor; check diagonals and symmetry.

outcome: The base frame begins as a square, stiff platform.

tasks.desc: Bolt/glue/screw chassis base using temporary clamps; measure diagonals before tightening.
Add initial cross-bracing and mark locations for axle mounts.

outcome: Axle mounts are positioned before irreversible fastening.

tasks.desc: Position front/rear axle lines on frame and check wheel clearance, toe, and body overhang.
tasks.question: Are axle positions final?

tasks.desc: Fabricate or mock up axle brackets/gussets with cardboard/ply before metal/wood final.

outcome: Steering geometry becomes physical.

tasks.desc: Install/mock front axle and steering column support; avoid looseness or flex.
Check steering travel, hand clearance, wheel clearance, and no over-centre binding.
Take build photos for private log; mark anything unsafe before public sharing.

outcome: The rear end can carry load and accept braking.

tasks.desc: Install/mock rear axle and wheel bearings; check parallel alignment and bearing spin.
Fit brake mount or drag-brake bracket; verify clear cable/lever path.

outcome: The chassis is ready for first low-speed roll checks.

tasks.desc: Mount temporary seat/driver ballast and check driver reach to steering/brakes.
Prepare low-speed test checklist: helmet/PPE, spotter, flat surface, brake zone, no traffic.

outcome: The chassis rolls, steers, and stops slowly.

tasks.desc: Perform flat-ground push test with safety gear; speed walking pace only.
Log steering feel, brake response, wheel rub, frame flex, loose fasteners.

outcome: The first defects are corrected before bodywork hides them.

tasks.desc: Fix looseness, rub, bracket flex, or brake weakness found in flat test.
Retest flat roll and brake stop; update issue status.

outcome: The chassis survives a simple proof load before speed.

tasks.desc: Load chassis with driver-equivalent mass plus margin; inspect flex, cracks, bolt movement.
Mark reinforcement points and update CAD/BOM as-built differences.

outcome: Driver protection is designed before body spectacle.

tasks.desc: Mock roll/side protection, harness/seat retention, and cockpit padding; verify exit path.
tasks.question: What minimum roll/side protection is being built?

tasks.desc: Source padding/harness/helmet fit materials; update BOM.

outcome: Critical chassis areas get reinforced.

tasks.desc: Install reinforcement at axle mounts, steering column support, seat mount, brake mount.
Torque/check all fasteners; add locknuts/threadlocker where appropriate.

outcome: Progressively test only after flat tests pass.

tasks.desc: Perform very low-slope coast test with spotter and runout; no aggressive steering.
Tune brake lever travel and tyre pressure; log stop distance and steering stability.

outcome: Cosmetic body mounts cannot compromise steering/braking.

tasks.desc: Design removable/sacrificial body mounting tabs; keep clear of wheels, steering, brake cable.
Transfer body panel dimensions to cardboard templates.

outcome: Heater silhouette appears without high mass.

tasks.desc: Build lightweight body skeleton with correx/foam/cardboard templates; keep upper body hollow/light.
Check body does not interfere with driver exit, steering range, or brake access.

outcome: The raked/boat-tailed idea survives contact with materials.

tasks.desc: Fit raked front/heater profile and rear taper/pod; keep surfaces smooth and removable.
Compare as-built body to CFD-winning geometry; log deviations and risks.

outcome: The effect is tested separately before mounting.

tasks.desc: Prototype chosen glow mechanism on bench: panel flip/fabric flutter/manual reveal.
Run 10 repeat cycles; log jams, reset time, and failure points.

outcome: The effect cannot jam critical systems.

tasks.desc: Plan mounting route for glow effect with positive stops, retention, and wheel/steering clearance.
Decision: keep automatic effect, switch to manual backup, or simplify.
tasks.question: Is the effect reliable enough for the cart?

outcome: Body and effect are mounted lightly and safely.

tasks.desc: Mount body panels with service access and sacrificial breakaway logic.
Mount glow effect and run push-test airflow/manual trigger tests.
Capture private build update; decide whether public teaser is still premature.
tasks.question: Public teaser now or wait until rolling tests pass?

outcome: Check the whole cart before adding paint/livery.

tasks.desc: Run inspection-style checklist: size, weight estimate, no battery/stored energy, steering/brakes, clearances.
Remove or redesign any decoration/body pieces that can jam wheels or steering.

outcome: Finish work stays lightweight and removable.

tasks.desc: Sand/trim body panels, tape seams, and mark access panels; do not permanently seal critical areas.
Draft livery layout with sponsor-safe placeholder zones and no logo commitment until approved.

outcome: Performance and safety gear work together.

tasks.desc: Fit helmet/protective clothing inside gas-cylinder costume concept; verify vision and movement.
Write/refine 30-second start-line skit with roles for up to five team members.

outcome: You catch overweight risk before paint and extras.

tasks.desc: Weigh rolling chassis/body/components separately; compare to 80 kg max excluding driver.
Make cut/remove/rebuild list for heavy or high-mounted components.
tasks.question: Where can weight be removed with least safety penalty?

outcome: Inspection-critical systems get a dedicated audit.

tasks.desc: Inspect steering fasteners, column support, tie rods, wheel clearance, and full-lock behavior.
Inspect brake mounts, cables, lever travel, pad contact, and backup drag-brake if installed.

outcome: The cart starts looking like the concept without hiding hazards.

tasks.desc: Apply base colors/graphics lightly; keep access to fasteners, brakes, and steering joints.
Photograph cart from judge/crowd angles; check one-second recognisability again.

outcome: Controlled testing replaces race-day surprises.

tasks.desc: Perform flat tow/push tests at increasing speed with full PPE and spotter.
Log steering wobble, brake stop distance, panel movement, effect reliability, and loose items.
Film short private clips for future build story; keep failures as useful lessons.

outcome: Failures are fixed while there is still calendar space.

tasks.desc: Repair issues from tow/flat tests; prioritize steering, brakes, axle mounts, and loose bodywork.
Retest the fixed items and update issue status.

outcome: Real-world drag/rolling data starts.

tasks.desc: Find safe, legal, controlled low-grade slope for repeatable coast-down tests; define start/finish markers.
Run baseline coast-down with current body and tyre pressure; record time, GPS speed, notes.

outcome: Speed tuning is done with data, not guesswork.

tasks.desc: Run tyre pressure comparison: firm/medium/soft within safe limits; record time and handling.
Test low-mounted ballast only if structure and braking remain safe; log effect on speed/stability.
tasks.question: Does ballast help without hurting braking/handling?

outcome: Flow separation gets checked physically.

tasks.desc: Tape short wool tufts to body and film a low-speed tow/coast test.
Identify thrashing tufts/separation zones and decide if simple tape/deflector changes are worth it.
tasks.question: Which aero fixes are simple enough to implement?

outcome: The cart must survive repeated runs.

tasks.desc: Run 20-cycle steering/brake/effect check in workshop; log any looseness or jamming.
Create pre-run and post-run checklists for every future test day.

outcome: Branding does not create a late compliance problem.

tasks.desc: Confirm sponsor status and required Red Bull approval process before applying final logos.
tasks.question: Any sponsor identity to submit for approval now?

tasks.desc: Prepare no-sponsor livery fallback that still looks complete.

outcome: Real handling is checked with controlled escalation.

tasks.desc: Run medium-slope test only if pre-run checklist passes; full PPE, spotters, clear runout.
Check braking under load, steering wobble, body vibration, effect retention, and driver confidence.
Record clean progress clip only if safe and non-sensitive; save as possible build-in-public asset.

outcome: No known serious issue carries forward.

tasks.desc: Repair or redesign all P0 issues found in medium-slope test.
Update as-built drawings and BOM to match the cart after fixes.

outcome: Performance becomes repeatable.

tasks.desc: Rehearse skit standing still with timer; cut to under 45 seconds, then target 30 seconds.
Assign props, cues, and who pushes/does what before start line.

outcome: The cockpit/driver safety kit is not optional.

tasks.desc: Finalize padding, helmet clearance, gloves/closed shoes/long sleeves/trousers compatibility.
Check driver can exit quickly and nothing snags on costume/bodywork.

outcome: Free speed and reliability get a quiet engineering pass.

tasks.desc: Clean/lube/inspect bearings and verify wheel spin; replace rough bearings if needed.
Fastener audit 2: locknuts, washers, axle/brake/steering mounts, body breakaway points.

outcome: You test against inspection before inspection tests you.

tasks.desc: Run full pre-tech checklist: dimensions, weight, no battery/stored energy, brakes, steering, safety gear.
Photograph evidence for each checklist item and store it in /06_buildlog.

outcome: The cart reads clearly at speed and in photos.

tasks.desc: Apply final non-sensitive livery/logos only after approvals; otherwise use fallback graphics.
Run one-second recognition test with 3 people; adjust if the gas-heater read is weak.

outcome: Cart, driver, costume, and skit are tested together.

tasks.desc: Run full pre-run checklist, skit rehearsal, push/start sequence, low/medium coast test.
Log every issue with severity and owner; no P0/P1 issue can remain unassigned.
Create first public-story draft if Gate 2 passes; keep it private until safe to publish.
tasks.question: Is the project ready to show publicly?

outcome: A deliberate buffer prevents rushed mistakes.

tasks.desc: Fix dress rehearsal issues; prioritize anything affecting stopping, steering, driver safety, or structural integrity.
Retest only the fixed systems; avoid introducing new cosmetic changes unless necessary.

outcome: Anyone on the team can understand the cart.

tasks.desc: Assemble as-built pack: drawings, BOM, photos, checklist, known spares/tools list.
Create race/test-day tool kit list: spanners, spare bolts, tape, zip ties, pump, brake cable, first aid.

outcome: Logistics stop being a hidden blocker.

tasks.desc: Source spares: fasteners, cable ties, brake cable/pads, wheel tube/bearing if possible.
Plan transport/storage: cart dimensions, loading method, straps, weather protection, helpers.
tasks.question: How will the finished cart be moved safely?

focus: Final medium test or controlled static review
outcome: Choose data or restraint based on risk.

tasks.desc: If safe/legal, run final controlled medium test; otherwise run static/brake/steering inspection only.
tasks.question: Run another test or preserve the cart?

tasks.desc: Record final handling/stopping/effect notes and freeze tuning settings.

outcome: Stop tinkering unless safety requires it.

tasks.desc: Declare build freeze: only safety fixes and approved reliability fixes after this point.
tasks.question: Is the cart stable enough to freeze?

tasks.desc: Label removable panels, tools, spares, and setup steps so helpers can assist quickly.

outcome: Build-in-public can start only if it helps, not distracts.

tasks.desc: Review public story assets: safe clips, lessons learned, sponsor approvals, no private risk info.
tasks.question: Launch follow-our-story now, later, or after selection confirmation?

tasks.desc: Prepare simple update template: what changed, what was tested, next milestone, one photo/clip.

outcome: Make it look good without making it worse.

tasks.desc: Light polish/paint touch-ups only where they cannot hide defects or add significant weight.
Final fastener, steering, brake, wheel, body, effect, and PPE checklist run.

outcome: Everyone knows the runbook.

tasks.desc: Rehearse start-line roles, push sequence, driver entry/exit, and emergency stop communication.
Create contingency list: what to cut/remove/repair quickly if inspection flags something.

outcome: The project moves into race-month maintenance mode.

tasks.desc: Create September maintenance/test calendar with weekly checks and no major redesigns.
Archive August design/build progress and prepare a clean summary for teammates/sponsors/public later.
